deepDive.title: DemoPSD: Disagreement-Modulated Policy Self-Distillation
deepDive.summary: This paper is a inference systems lead from the research stream. On-policy self-distillation (OPSD) has emerged as a practical method for training large language models (LLMs) to reason, where a single model acts as both the teacher and the student with different levels of information access. It gets the longer read because the public context is source-grounded and the item is close to the systems layer.
bullets: What happened: This paper is a inference systems lead from the research stream. Why it matters: The systems angle is inference systems; the useful question is whether the method changes reliability, runtime behavior, governance, or infrastructure constraints. The public metadata is strong enough for a compact self-contained read.
